Tidy Tuesday - Africa Mapping (2021-11-09)

15 November 2021

An overview map of the African continent with country capitals

tm_shape(africountries) +
  tm_borders(col = "grey") +
  tm_fill() +
  tm_shape(africapitals) +
    tm_dots(col = "red", size = "pop") +
    tm_text("capitalname") +
  tm_layout(
    title = "Africa",
    bg.color = "lightblue"
    )
Legend labels were too wide. Therefore, legend.text.size has been set to 0.57. Increase legend.width (argument of tm_layout) to make the legend wider and therefore the labels larger.
The legend is too narrow to place all symbol sizes.

Zooming in on Nigeria


nigeria <- africountries %>% 
  filter(iso_a3 == "NGA")

abuja <- africapitals %>% 
  filter(iso3c == "NGA")

highways <- afrihighway %>% 
  filter(st_intersects(x = ., y = nigeria, sparse = FALSE))
st_as_s2(): dropping Z and/or M coordinate
  
tm_shape(nigeria) +
  tm_polygons(col = "white") +
  tm_borders(col = "black") +
  tm_shape(abuja) +
    tm_dots(col = "red", size = 1) +
    tm_text("capitalname") +
  tm_shape(highways) +
    tm_lines(col = "blue") +
  tm_layout(
    title = "Nigera",
    bg.color = "grey"
  )
Warning: One tm layer group has duplicated layer types, which are omitted. To draw multiple layers of the same type, use multiple layer groups (i.e. specify tm_shape prior to each of them).

Nigeria with a bounding box approach

bb <- st_bbox(nigeria)
bbox <- st_as_sfc(bb)
bb_highways <- afrihighway %>% 
  filter(st_intersects(x = ., y = bbox, sparse = FALSE))
st_as_s2(): dropping Z and/or M coordinate
tm_shape(bbox) +
  tm_polygons() +
  tm_shape(bb_highways) +
    tm_lines() +
  tm_shape(nigeria) +
    tm_polygons(col = "darkblue", alpha = 0.5)

NA
Mock, Thomas. 2021. “Tidy Tuesday: A Weekly Data Project Aimed at the r Ecosystem.” https://github.com/rfordatascience/tidytuesday.
R Core Team. 2019. R: A Language and Environment for Statistical Computing. Vienna, Austria: R Foundation for Statistical Computing. https://www.R-project.org.
